message:
docs: add comprehensive MoE implementation TODO

Create detailed task list for MoE inference:
- Phase 1: Download model and test framework
- Phase 2: Implement forward pass with dequantization
- Phase 3: Full inference pipeline
- Alternative hybrid approach with llama.cpp
- Expected performance targets
- Success criteria and next steps

This provides clear roadmap for completing MoE inference implementation.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,292 @@
+# MoE Implementation TODO
+
+## Current Status: Framework Complete ✅
+
+**What's Working:**
+- ✅ MoE detection from GGUF metadata
+- ✅ Native Zig MoE core (routing, FFN, weight loading)
+- ✅ GGUF loader with weight access
+- ✅ Tokenization via llama.cpp
+- ✅ Layer-split optimization (dense + MoE)
+- ✅ Test framework compiles successfully
+
+**What's Missing:**
+- ❌ Actual model file (Mixtral 46GB)
+- ❌ Forward pass implementation
+- ❌ Real performance measurements
+
+---
+
+## Phase 1: Download Model & Test (30 minutes)
+
+### Task 1.1: Download Mixtral Model
+```bash
+cd /Users/steven_chong/Downloads/repos/zell
+
+# Create models directory if needed
+mkdir -p models
+
+# Download Mixtral 8x7B Q8_0 (~46GB, ~30 min on fast connection)
+wget https://huggingface.co/TheBloke/Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q8_0.gguf \
+  -O models/mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q8_0.gguf
+
+# Verify download
+ls -lh models/mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q8_0.gguf
+# Should be ~46GB
+```
+
+### Task 1.2: Test Current Framework
+```bash
+# Run basic test (framework overhead only)
+./zig-out/bin/zell run models/mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q8_0.gguf "What is 2+2?"
+
+# Expected output:
+# - Model loads successfully
+# - Tokenizes prompt
+# - Loads MoE weights for layer 0
+# - Reports framework overhead (~0ms, not real inference)
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 2: Implement Forward Pass (2-4 hours)
+
+### Task 2.1: Dequantize Router Weights
+**File:** `src/moe_inference.zig`
+
+```zig
+// In runMoEInference, after loading layer_weights:
+
+// Dequantize router weights (Q8_0 -> FP32)
+const router_size = prof.model_info.n_embd * prof.model_info.n_expert;
+var router_f32 = try allocator.alloc(f32, router_size);
+defer allocator.free(router_f32);
+
+// Use GGUF loader to dequantize
+const router_bf16 = try allocator.alloc(gguf_loader.BF16, router_size);
+defer allocator.free(router_bf16);
+try loader.dequantizeToBF16("blk.0.ffn_gate_inp.weight", router_bf16);
+
+// Convert BF16 -> FP32
+for (router_bf16, 0..) |bf16_val, i| {
+    router_f32[i] = bf16ToF32(bf16_val);
+}
+```
+
+### Task 2.2: Dequantize Expert Weights
+```zig
+// Dequantize all 8 experts for layer 0
+var expert_gate_f32 = try allocator.alloc([]f32, 8);
+var expert_up_f32 = try allocator.alloc([]f32, 8);
+var expert_down_f32 = try allocator.alloc([]f32, 8);
+
+defer {
+    for (expert_gate_f32) |weights| allocator.free(weights);
+    for (expert_up_f32) |weights| allocator.free(weights);
+    for (expert_down_f32) |weights| allocator.free(weights);
+    allocator.free(expert_gate_f32);
+    allocator.free(expert_up_f32);
+    allocator.free(expert_down_f32);
+}
+
+const n_ff = 14336; // Mixtral intermediate size
+const n_embd = 4096; // Mixtral hidden size
+
+for (0..8) |expert_idx| {
+    // Dequantize gate weights
+    expert_gate_f32[expert_idx] = try allocator.alloc(f32, n_ff * n_embd);
+    const gate_bf16 = try allocator.alloc(gguf_loader.BF16, n_ff * n_embd);
+    defer allocator.free(gate_bf16);
+
+    var buf: [128]u8 = undefined;
+    const gate_name = try std.fmt.bufPrint(&buf, "blk.0.ffn_gate.{d}.weight", .{expert_idx});
+    try loader.dequantizeToBF16(gate_name, gate_bf16);
+
+    for (gate_bf16, 0..) |val, i| {
+        expert_gate_f32[expert_idx][i] = bf16ToF32(val);
+    }
+
+    // Repeat for up and down...
+}
+```
+
+### Task 2.3: Run MoE FFN
+```zig
+// Create MoE config
+const moe_config = moe.MoEConfig{
+    .n_expert = 8,
+    .n_expert_used = 2, // Mixtral uses top-2
+    .n_embd = 4096,
+    .n_ff = 14336,
+};
+
+// Run MoE FFN for single layer
+const output = try moe.moeFFN(
+    allocator,
+    hidden_states, // [n_embd]
+    router_f32,
+    expert_gate_f32,
+    expert_up_f32,
+    expert_down_f32,
+    moe_config,
+    1, // n_tokens = 1
+);
+defer allocator.free(output);
+```
+
+### Task 2.4: Measure Performance
+```zig
+// Wrap in timing loop
+const n_iterations: usize = 10;
+const start = std.time.nanoTimestamp();
+
+for (0..n_iterations) |_| {
+    const result = try moe.moeFFN(...);
+    allocator.free(result);
+}
+
+const end = std.time.nanoTimestamp();
+const elapsed_ms = @divTrunc(end - start, 1_000_000);
+const time_per_token = @divTrunc(elapsed_ms, n_iterations);
+
+std.debug.print("⏱️  MoE FFN Performance (single layer):\n", .{});
+std.debug.print("   Time per token: {} ms\n", .{time_per_token});
+std.debug.print("   Throughput: {} tok/s\n", .{@divTrunc(1000, time_per_token)});
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3: Full Inference Pipeline (4-8 hours)
+
+### Task 3.1: Load Token Embeddings
+**Complexity:** Medium
+- Load `token_embd.weight` from GGUF
+- Implement embedding lookup for input tokens
+- Return initial hidden states [n_embd, n_tokens]
+
+### Task 3.2: Implement Layer Loop
+**Complexity:** High
+- For each of 32 layers:
+  - Load layer weights (attention + MoE FFN)
+  - Run attention (use llama.cpp or implement native)
+  - Run MoE FFN (our native implementation)
+  - Apply RMSNorm
+- Progressive: Start with 1 layer, then 8, then all 32
+
+### Task 3.3: Output Projection & Sampling
+**Complexity:** Medium
+- Apply output norm
+- LM head projection (hidden → vocab logits)
+- Sample next token (greedy or temperature-based)
+- Detokenize for display
+
+### Task 3.4: Generation Loop
+**Complexity:** Medium
+- Repeat forward pass for max_tokens
+- Update KV cache (if implementing attention)
+- Display generated text token by token
+
+---
+
+## Expected Performance Targets
+
+### Single Layer (MoE FFN only):
+- **CPU (no optimization):** ~200-500ms per token
+- **With SIMD dequant:** ~50-100ms per token
+- **Target:** < 100ms per layer
+
+### Full 32 Layers:
+- **Without layer-split:** ~3-6 seconds per token (0.15-0.3 tok/s)
+- **With layer-split (8 draft, 24 target):**
+  - Draft: ~1 second (fast, GPU-resident)
+  - Target: ~2-4 seconds (SSD-streamed)
+  - **Combined: ~0.3-0.5 tok/s** (2-3 seconds per token)
+
+### Comparison to Ollama:
+- **Ollama:** Cannot run Mixtral on 16GB Mac (OOM)
+- **Zell:** Runs with layer-split at ~0.3-0.5 tok/s
+- **Key advantage:** Can run the model at all!
+
+---
+
+## Alternative Approach: Hybrid with llama.cpp
+
+Instead of implementing full transformer from scratch, consider:
+1. Use llama.cpp for attention + everything except MoE FFN
+2. Hook into llama.cpp graph building
+3. Replace MoE FFN nodes with our native implementation
+4. Let llama.cpp handle KV cache, sampling, etc.
+
+**Pros:**
+- Faster to implement (days vs weeks)
+- Reuse battle-tested code
+- Focus effort on MoE optimization
+
+**Cons:**
+- Less control over full pipeline
+- Need to understand llama.cpp internals
+- May be harder to optimize layer-split
+
+---
+
+## Decision Point
+
+**Choose one path:**
+
+### Path A: Full Native Implementation (Weeks of work)
+- Complete control over pipeline
+- Learn everything about transformer inference
+- Easier to optimize layer-split later
+- More code to maintain
+
+### Path B: Hybrid (Hook into llama.cpp) (Days of work)
+- Faster time to working inference
+- Reuse existing infrastructure
+- Focus on MoE optimization only
+- Requires understanding llama.cpp graph API
+
+**Recommendation:** Start with Path A for single-layer test, then evaluate if hybrid makes sense for full pipeline.
+
+---
+
+## Files to Modify
+
+### Core Implementation:
+- `src/moe_inference.zig` - Main inference loop
+- `src/moe.zig` - May need optimizations
+
+### Supporting Files:
+- `src/inference/gguf_loader.zig` - Weight loading helpers (already done)
+- `src/inference/dequantize_simd.zig` - Dequantization (already done)
+- `src/main.zig` - Entry point routing (already done)
+
+### Testing:
+- Create `src/test_moe_ffn.zig` - Unit test for single MoE layer
+- Add benchmarks to measure tok/s
+
+---
+
+## Success Criteria
+
+**Minimum Viable:**
+- ✅ Load Mixtral model
+- ✅ Tokenize input
+- ✅ Run MoE FFN for single layer
+- ✅ Measure time per token
+- ✅ Report tok/s
+
+**Full Implementation:**
+- Run all 32 layers
+- Generate coherent text
+- Achieve > 0.3 tok/s on 16GB Mac
+- Faster than Ollama (which can't run it at all!)
+
+---
+
+## Current Blockers
+
+1. **No model file** - Need to download Mixtral (~46GB)
+2. **Forward pass not implemented** - Need dequantization + moeFFN call
+3. **No performance baseline** - Can't measure until forward pass works
+
+**Next immediate task:** Download model and test current framework!
